# terrible_netapp_cloudmanager_na_cloudmanager_connector_gcp (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `company` (String) The name of the company of the user.
- `gcp_service_account_email` (String) The email of the service_account for the connector instance. This service account is used to allow the Connector to create Cloud Volume ONTAP.
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the Cloud Manager connector for GCP to manage.
- `project_id` (String) The GCP project_id where the connector will be created.
- `zone` (String) The GCP zone where the Connector will be created.

### Optional

- `account_id` (String) The NetApp account ID that the Connector will be associated with. If not provided, Cloud Manager uses the first account. If no account exists, Cloud Manager creates a new account. You can find the account ID in the account tab of Cloud Manager at [https://cloudmanager.netapp.com](https://cloudmanager.netapp.com).
- `associate_public_ip` (Boolean) Indicates whether to associate a public IP address to the virtual machine.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `client_id` (String) The client ID of the Cloud Manager Connector. The connector ID. If state is absent, the client id is used to identify the agent and delete it. If state is absent and this parameter is not set, all agents associated with `name` are deleted. Ignored when state is present.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `firewall_tags` (Boolean) Indicates whether to add firewall_tags to the connector VM (HTTP and HTTP).
- `gcp_service_account_path` (String) The local path of the service_account JSON file for GCP authorization purposes. This service account is used to create the Connector in GCP.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `machine_type` (String) The machine_type for the Connector VM.
- `network_project_id` (String) The project id in GCP associated with the Subnet. If not provided, it is assumed that the Subnet is within the previously specified project id.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `proxy_certificates` (String) The proxy certificates. A list of certificate file names.
- `proxy_password` (String) The proxy password, if using a proxy to connect to the internet.
- `proxy_url` (String) The proxy URL, if using a proxy to connect to the internet.
- `proxy_user_name` (String) The proxy user name, if using a proxy to connect to the internet.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Whether the specified Cloud Manager connector for GCP should exist or not.
- `subnet_id` (String) The name of the subnet for the virtual machine.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `client_ids` (String) a list of client ids matching the name and provider if the connector already exists. ideally the list should be empty, or contain a single element matching client_id.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
